paper_title: Predicting Seasonally Varying Global Species-Specific Depth Habitat of Planktonic Foraminifera

methods.description: Modification of the PLAFOM model to include light dependency and integration with CESM1.2(BGC) to predict the vertical distribution of planktonic foraminifera.

steps.description: Update PLAFOM by including light dependency for symbiont-bearing planktonic foraminifera.

steps.description: Apply spatial parameterization of carbon biomass as a function of temperature, nutrition, competition, and light on depth-resolved parameter fields.

steps.description: Combine PLAFOM2.0 with CESM1.2(BGC) to resolve the vertical dimension throughout the ocean.

methods.description: Conducting a preindustrial control experiment to test the model by simulating ocean and sea ice states and initializing biogeochemical tracer fields.

steps.description: Spin up the model from rest for 300 years using climatological forcing.

steps.description: Initialize biogeochemical tracer fields from climatologies.
Integrate the CESM1.2(BGC+PLA) preindustrial control simulation for 300 years.

methods.description: Comparison of model predictions with fossil data using core-top samples to examine spatial patterns of planktonic foraminiferal species.
